effect_size: 1.4x T2D risk per T allele (most important T2D gene)
mechanism: Impaired beta-cell function via Wnt signaling pathway disruption. TCF7L2 encodes transcription factor 7-like 2, critical for insulin secretion regulation and incretin signaling.
disease_associations: Type 2 Diabetes, impaired glucose tolerance, gestational diabetes
text_chunk: TCF7L2 rs7903146 is the strongest common genetic risk factor for type 2 diabetes. Each T allele increases risk ~1.4x through impaired pancreatic beta-cell function. TT homozygotes warrant tighter HbA1c and glucose monitoring thresholds (<5.5% HbA1c, <95 mg/dL fasting glucose).

effect_size: Pro12Ala (G allele) reduces T2D risk by ~20%. CC (Pro/Pro) is the risk genotype.
mechanism: PPARG encodes peroxisome proliferator-activated receptor gamma, a nuclear receptor controlling adipogenesis and insulin sensitivity. Ala12 variant reduces receptor activity, paradoxically improving insulin sensitivity.
disease_associations: Type 2 Diabetes, insulin resistance, metabolic syndrome
text_chunk: PPARG rs1801282 Pro12Ala: the G allele (Ala) is protective, reducing T2D risk ~20%. The common CC genotype (Pro/Pro) confers standard risk. PPARG is the target of thiazolidinediones (pioglitazone). Ala carriers may respond differently to TZD therapy.

effect_size: 1.15x T2D risk per C allele. Loss-of-function variants are protective.
mechanism: SLC30A8 encodes zinc transporter ZnT8, which transports zinc into insulin secretory granules in pancreatic beta-cells. The C allele (Arg325) impairs zinc-insulin crystallization.
disease_associations: Type 2 Diabetes, impaired insulin processing
text_chunk: SLC30A8 rs13266634 encodes the ZnT8 zinc transporter in pancreatic beta-cells. C allele (Arg325) increases T2D risk 1.15x. Loss-of-function variants in SLC30A8 are paradoxically protective against T2D, making this a therapeutic target. Zinc supplementation may partially compensate.

effect_size: 1.15x T2D risk per T allele (Lys23 variant)
mechanism: KCNJ11 encodes the Kir6.2 subunit of the pancreatic beta-cell ATP-sensitive potassium channel. The T allele (Glu23Lys) reduces channel sensitivity to ATP, impairing glucose-stimulated insulin secretion.
disease_associations: Type 2 Diabetes, neonatal diabetes (activating mutations), hyperinsulinism (loss-of-function)
text_chunk: KCNJ11 rs5219 Glu23Lys: T allele (Lys23) increases T2D risk 1.15x by reducing ATP sensitivity of beta-cell KATP channels. Sulfonylureas (glimepiride, glyburide) act directly on this channel. Rare activating mutations cause neonatal diabetes responsive to sulfonylureas instead of insulin.

effect_size: T allele increases fasting glucose ~0.03 mmol/L and triglycerides ~0.1 mmol/L
mechanism: GCKR encodes glucokinase regulatory protein. The T allele reduces GCKR inhibition of glucokinase in the liver, increasing hepatic glucose uptake but also increasing de novo lipogenesis and triglyceride production.
disease_associations: Type 2 Diabetes, hypertriglyceridemia, NAFLD
text_chunk: GCKR rs780094 T allele reduces glucokinase regulation, increasing hepatic glucose uptake but paradoxically raising triglycerides via de novo lipogenesis. TT homozygotes have higher fasting triglycerides and should be monitored for NAFLD. Pleiotropic effects complicate interpretation.

effect_size: E4: 3x AD risk (heterozygous), 15x (homozygous). LDL +10-15 mg/dL per E4 allele. E2: lower LDL, higher TG.
mechanism: APOE encodes apolipoprotein E, critical for lipoprotein clearance via hepatic LDL receptor. E4 has reduced receptor binding affinity and impairs amyloid-beta clearance in the brain. E2 has highest receptor affinity.
disease_associations: Alzheimer disease, cardiovascular disease, type III hyperlipoproteinemia (E2/E2)
text_chunk: APOE genotype: E3/E3 is reference. E4 carriers: LDL +10-15 mg/dL per allele, 3x Alzheimer risk (E3/E4), 15x (E4/E4). Target LDL <70 mg/dL for E4 carriers. E2/E2 causes type III hyperlipoproteinemia in some individuals. APOE genotyping affects both CV and dementia risk stratification.

effect_size: T allele (R46L loss-of-function): LDL reduced ~15-28%, CHD risk reduced ~47-88%
mechanism: PCSK9 promotes degradation of hepatic LDL receptors. Loss-of-function variants reduce PCSK9 activity, preserving LDL receptors and increasing LDL clearance. This is the basis for PCSK9 inhibitor drugs (evolocumab, alirocumab).
disease_associations: Hypercholesterolemia (gain-of-function), reduced cardiovascular risk (loss-of-function)
text_chunk: PCSK9 rs11591147 R46L: T allele is a natural loss-of-function variant that lowers lifetime LDL ~15-28% and reduces CHD risk by 47-88%. This Mendelian randomization evidence validated PCSK9 as a drug target. PCSK9 inhibitors (evolocumab/FOURIER trial, alirocumab) reduce LDL 50-60% and CV events 15-20%.

effect_size: GG: 3.2x NAFLD risk, 5.3x NASH risk, hepatic fat content +73%
mechanism: PNPLA3 encodes patatin-like phospholipase domain-containing protein 3 (adiponutrin). The I148M variant (G allele) impairs triglyceride hydrolysis and retinol metabolism in hepatocytes, causing lipid accumulation and hepatic stellate cell activation.
disease_associations: NAFLD, NASH, liver fibrosis, hepatocellular carcinoma, alcoholic liver disease
text_chunk: PNPLA3 rs738409 I148M is the strongest genetic risk factor for NAFLD. GG homozygotes have 3.2x NAFLD risk with ~73% more hepatic fat. ALT baseline is ~10-15 U/L higher in GG carriers - use adjusted thresholds. Monitor with FIB-4 annually and FibroScan. HSD17B13 loss-of-function partially mitigates PNPLA3 risk.

effect_size: T allele (E167K): 2.1x NAFLD risk, but lower LDL and reduced CV risk
mechanism: TM6SF2 encodes transmembrane 6 superfamily member 2, involved in VLDL secretion from hepatocytes. The E167K variant reduces VLDL secretion, trapping lipids in the liver (increasing NAFLD risk) but lowering circulating lipoproteins (reducing CV risk).
disease_associations: NAFLD, NASH, liver fibrosis, reduced cardiovascular risk (paradoxically)
text_chunk: TM6SF2 rs58542926 E167K: T allele increases NAFLD risk 2.1x by impairing hepatic VLDL secretion, trapping lipids in the liver. Paradoxically, this reduces circulating LDL and cardiovascular risk. TT carriers retain fat in liver rather than bloodstream - a hepatic vs cardiovascular risk trade-off.

effect_size: TA allele (splice variant): 17% reduced NASH risk, 30% reduced cirrhosis risk, 50% reduced HCC risk per allele
mechanism: HSD17B13 encodes hydroxysteroid 17-beta dehydrogenase 13, a hepatic lipid droplet enzyme. The TA insertion causes a splice variant producing a truncated, inactive protein. Loss of function reduces hepatocellular inflammation and fibrosis progression.
disease_associations: Protective against NASH, liver fibrosis, cirrhosis, hepatocellular carcinoma
text_chunk: HSD17B13 rs72613567 TA insertion is a protective loss-of-function variant. Each TA allele reduces NASH risk 17%, cirrhosis risk 30%, and HCC risk 50%. Particularly protective in PNPLA3 GG carriers, partially mitigating their elevated liver risk. HSD17B13 is an active drug target (ARO-HSD, RNAi therapy).

effect_size: TT: ~70% reduced enzyme activity, 25% higher homocysteine, neural tube defect risk 2x
mechanism: MTHFR encodes methylenetetrahydrofolate reductase, which converts 5,10-methyleneTHF to 5-methylTHF (active folate). C677T (Ala222Val) reduces thermostability and activity, impairing one-carbon metabolism, DNA methylation, and homocysteine remethylation.
disease_associations: Hyperhomocysteinemia, neural tube defects, cardiovascular disease, depression, recurrent pregnancy loss
text_chunk: MTHFR rs1801133 C677T: TT homozygotes have ~70% reduced enzyme activity and ~25% higher homocysteine. Must use L-methylfolate (not folic acid) as TT carriers cannot efficiently convert folic acid. Dose: 1000 mcg L-methylfolate daily. Avoid folic acid supplementation. Monitor homocysteine (target <10 umol/L).

effect_size: AA (Thr92Ala): impaired T4-to-T3 conversion, ~1.3x hypothyroid symptoms on T4-only therapy
mechanism: DIO2 encodes type 2 iodothyronine deiodinase, the primary enzyme converting T4 to active T3 in peripheral tissues (brain, muscle, thyroid). Thr92Ala reduces enzyme velocity, leading to lower tissue T3 despite normal serum levels.
disease_associations: Persistent hypothyroid symptoms on levothyroxine, impaired T4-to-T3 conversion, psychological well-being on T4
text_chunk: DIO2 rs225014 Thr92Ala: AA carriers have impaired T4-to-T3 conversion and may remain symptomatic on levothyroxine (T4) monotherapy. May benefit from combination T4/T3 therapy (liothyronine). Optimal TSH target for AA carriers: 0.5-1.5 mIU/L. Free T3/Free T4 ratio <0.25 confirms impaired conversion.

effect_size: CC: ~25-40% reduced delta-5 desaturase activity, lower EPA/DHA from ALA conversion
mechanism: FADS1 encodes fatty acid desaturase 1 (delta-5 desaturase), which catalyzes the conversion of DGLA to arachidonic acid and EPA to DHA in the omega-3 pathway. CC carriers have reduced conversion efficiency.
disease_associations: Lower omega-3 index, altered inflammatory response, cardiovascular risk modification
text_chunk: FADS1 rs174546: CC carriers have 25-40% reduced delta-5 desaturase activity. Cannot efficiently convert plant-based ALA (flaxseed, walnuts) to EPA/DHA. Must supplement with preformed EPA+DHA (fish oil 2-4g daily). Target Omega-3 Index >8%. Omega-3 Index reference ranges should be genotype-adjusted.

effect_size: C282Y homozygous (AA): ~80% develop iron overload, clinical penetrance ~30-50%
mechanism: HFE encodes the hereditary hemochromatosis protein, which regulates hepcidin expression. C282Y disrupts HFE-beta2 microglobulin interaction, reducing hepcidin production and increasing intestinal iron absorption.
disease_associations: Hereditary hemochromatosis, liver cirrhosis, diabetes, cardiomyopathy, arthritis, hepatocellular carcinoma
text_chunk: HFE rs1800562 C282Y: AA homozygotes have hereditary hemochromatosis risk. Monitor ferritin and transferrin saturation every 6 months. Therapeutic phlebotomy when ferritin >300 ng/mL (male) or >200 (female). TSAT >45% triggers genotyping. C282Y/H63D compound heterozygotes have milder risk.

effect_size: H63D modifier: compound heterozygosity (C282Y/H63D) confers moderate iron overload risk
mechanism: H63D (His63Asp) is a milder HFE variant that partially impairs hepcidin regulation. Alone, H63D homozygosity rarely causes clinical hemochromatosis. Combined with C282Y as compound heterozygote, it confers moderate risk.
disease_associations: Modifier for hereditary hemochromatosis, elevated ferritin, compound heterozygote risk
text_chunk: HFE rs1799945 H63D: G allele (Asp63) is a modifier variant. H63D/H63D homozygotes rarely develop clinical hemochromatosis. C282Y/H63D compound heterozygotes have ~1-2% risk of iron overload. Monitor ferritin annually if compound heterozygous. Ferritin should remain <300 ng/mL male, <200 female.

effect_size: AA: ~1.7x obesity risk, ~3 kg higher body weight, increased BMI ~0.4 kg/m2 per allele
mechanism: FTO encodes fat mass and obesity-associated protein, an RNA demethylase (m6A eraser). The risk variants increase IRX3 and IRX5 expression in adipocyte precursors, shifting thermogenesis from energy-dissipating to energy-storing programs.
disease_associations: Obesity, type 2 diabetes, cardiovascular disease, certain cancers
text_chunk: FTO rs9939609: AA carriers have 1.7x obesity risk and average ~3 kg higher body weight. FTO variants affect satiety signaling and energy expenditure through IRX3/IRX5 regulation. AA carriers may benefit from increased protein intake (higher satiety), structured meal timing, and more intensive exercise programs.

effect_size: Val158Met: Met/Met (AA) has 3-4x reduced COMT activity, higher dopamine and catecholamines
mechanism: COMT encodes catechol-O-methyltransferase, which degrades catecholamines (dopamine, norepinephrine, epinephrine) and catechol estrogens. Val158 (G) has high activity (warrior), Met158 (A) has low activity (worrier) with higher prefrontal dopamine.
disease_associations: Pain sensitivity, stress resilience, cognitive function, psychiatric disorders, estrogen metabolism
text_chunk: COMT rs4680 Val158Met: GG (Val/Val) 'Warrior' - high COMT activity, lower dopamine, stress resilient but lower baseline cognition. AA (Met/Met) 'Worrier' - low COMT activity, higher dopamine, better focus but higher anxiety. Affects pain sensitivity, estrogen metabolism (breast cancer risk), and response to stress.

effect_size: CC (FF genotype): reduced VDR transcriptional activity, may require higher vitamin D levels
mechanism: VDR encodes the vitamin D receptor, a nuclear receptor that mediates vitamin D effects on calcium metabolism, immune function, and cell differentiation. FokI polymorphism (rs2228570) affects translation start site, producing a shorter, potentially more active protein.
disease_associations: Vitamin D metabolism, osteoporosis risk, autoimmune disease susceptibility, cancer risk
text_chunk: VDR rs2228570 FokI: CC genotype may have reduced VDR signaling efficiency and require higher vitamin D levels (target 50-60 ng/mL vs standard 40-60). Affects calcium absorption, immune regulation, and cancer risk. VDR variants interact with vitamin D status - adequate levels are especially critical for CC carriers.

effect_size: GG (A66G Ile/Ile): ~4x reduced methionine synthase reductase activity, impaired B12 recycling
mechanism: MTRR encodes methionine synthase reductase, which regenerates the active form of vitamin B12 (methylcobalamin) needed by methionine synthase. A66G reduces enzyme activity, impairing homocysteine remethylation and one-carbon metabolism.
disease_associations: Elevated homocysteine, neural tube defects, B12 functional deficiency, cardiovascular risk
text_chunk: MTRR rs1801394 A66G: GG carriers have impaired methionine synthase reductase activity and reduced B12 recycling efficiency. Use methylcobalamin (not cyanocobalamin) form of B12. Target B12 >500 pg/mL (higher than standard cutoff). Combined MTHFR TT + MTRR GG significantly increases homocysteine.

effect_size: G allele associated with genetically higher baseline CRP levels (~15-20% increase)
mechanism: CRP encodes C-reactive protein. Promoter and coding variants affect transcription rate and protein levels independent of inflammation. This creates genetically determined baseline CRP variation that must be considered in CV risk assessment.
disease_associations: Genetically elevated CRP, modified cardiovascular risk interpretation
text_chunk: CRP rs1800947: G allele carriers have genetically higher baseline hs-CRP (~15-20% increase independent of inflammation). Important for accurate CV risk stratification - genetically elevated CRP may not carry the same CV risk as inflammation-elevated CRP. Mendelian randomization suggests CRP itself is not causal for CVD.

effect_size: GG: higher IL-6 levels, ~1.3x increased CV event risk, higher CRP
mechanism: IL6 encodes interleukin-6, the primary cytokine driving hepatic CRP production. The -174G>C promoter polymorphism affects IL6 transcription, with the G allele associated with higher IL-6 expression, particularly under inflammatory stimuli.
disease_associations: Cardiovascular disease, systemic inflammation, type 2 diabetes, Alzheimer disease
text_chunk: IL6 rs1800795 -174G>C: GG carriers have higher baseline IL-6 and CRP levels with ~1.3x increased CV risk. Unlike CRP, Mendelian randomization confirms IL-6 signaling is causal for CVD (tocilizumab reduces CV events). GG carriers may benefit from more aggressive anti-inflammatory strategies.

effect_size: GG: reduced delta-6 desaturase activity, impaired initial conversion of ALA to SDA and LA to GLA
mechanism: FADS2 encodes fatty acid desaturase 2 (delta-6 desaturase), the rate-limiting enzyme in omega-3 (ALA to EPA) and omega-6 (LA to GLA) conversion pathways. GG genotype reduces enzymatic efficiency.
disease_associations: Altered fatty acid profiles, modified omega-3/omega-6 metabolism, inflammatory response modulation
text_chunk: FADS2 rs1535: GG carriers have reduced delta-6 desaturase activity, the rate-limiting step in omega-3 and omega-6 conversion. Combined FADS1 CC + FADS2 GG severely impairs plant-based omega-3 utilization. These individuals require direct EPA+DHA supplementation (fish oil) rather than ALA (flaxseed).

effect_size: A allele: altered T3/T4 ratio, higher free T4, lower free T3
mechanism: DIO1 encodes type 1 iodothyronine deiodinase, which catalyzes outer-ring and inner-ring deiodination of thyroid hormones in liver and kidney. Variants affect T4-to-T3 conversion efficiency and T3 clearance.
disease_associations: Altered thyroid hormone metabolism, modified T3/T4 ratio
text_chunk: DIO1 rs2235544: A allele carriers have altered peripheral thyroid hormone metabolism with relatively higher T4 and lower T3. Combined DIO1 AA + DIO2 AA genotype has the most significant impairment in T4-to-T3 conversion. These individuals are most likely to benefit from combination T4/T3 therapy.

effect_size: Lp(a) levels >90% genetically determined. Low KIV-2 repeats = smaller apolipoprotein(a) = higher Lp(a) levels
mechanism: LPA gene contains a variable number of KIV-2 repeats (5-50+ copies). Fewer repeats produce a smaller apolipoprotein(a) protein that is secreted more efficiently, resulting in higher Lp(a) plasma levels. Lp(a) is both atherogenic and pro-thrombotic.
disease_associations: Cardiovascular disease, aortic valve stenosis, stroke, venous thromboembolism
text_chunk: LPA gene KIV-2 repeats determine >90% of Lp(a) variation. Low repeat number = high Lp(a) = elevated CV risk. Lp(a) >75 nmol/L (>30 mg/dL) is a causal, independent CV risk factor. Not reducible by lifestyle changes. Current therapies: PCSK9i (20-30% reduction), novel antisense (pelacarsen, 80% reduction in trials).

effect_size: 55-72% lifetime breast cancer risk, 39-44% lifetime ovarian cancer risk
mechanism: BRCA1 encodes breast cancer type 1 susceptibility protein, a tumor suppressor involved in homologous recombination DNA repair. Pathogenic variants impair double-strand break repair, leading to genomic instability and cancer predisposition.
disease_associations: Breast cancer, ovarian cancer, fallopian tube cancer, pancreatic cancer, prostate cancer
text_chunk: BRCA1 pathogenic variants (including rs80357906 5382insC): 55-72% lifetime breast cancer risk, 39-44% ovarian cancer risk. Management: enhanced screening (annual breast MRI from age 25), risk-reducing surgeries (mastectomy, oophorectomy), and PARP inhibitor therapy (olaparib) for BRCA-associated cancers. Cascade testing of family members essential.
